tiny_practice.action: Close your eyes and listen quietly, imagining God calling your name personally, just as He called Jeremiah

two_step.reflect_question: How do you respond when God challenges you to step beyond your perceived limitations?

options.response: Practice affirming God's empowerment instead of your weaknesses
Memorize verses about God's strength being made perfect in weakness
Take a concrete step of faith in an area where you feel unqualified

creative_prompt.instruction: Draw yourself as a 'fortified city, an iron pillar, and bronze wall' that God can use

reevaluation.question: How has your understanding of God's calling changed after reading this chapter?
options: Personal limitations don't restrict God's purpose

three_choice.question: Which aspect of divine calling speaks most profoundly to you?

choices.expansion: The divine understanding of our potential precedes our own self-awareness.
Divine purpose transcends human self-doubt and perceived inadequacies.
True spiritual calling demands radical obedience beyond personal comfort.

socratic_nudge.question: If God knows and shapes us before our first breath, how much of our life's trajectory is predetermined versus freely chosen?

moral_slider.question: How do you reconcile divine selection with human agency?

reevaluation.question: How has your understanding of personal calling shifted?
options: I recognize divine empowerment more deeply
I'm more open to unexpected paths

thematic_summaries.title: Divine Calling and Preparation
thematic_summaries.summary: God sovereignly calls Jeremiah to prophetic ministry before his birth, demonstrating divine election and purposeful preparation. The passage reveals God's intimate knowledge of human potential and His intentional selection of prophetic vessels.

thematic_summaries.title: Prophetic Commissioning
thematic_summaries.summary: Jeremiah receives a profound commissioning that involves both proclamation and spiritual warfare against systemic spiritual corruption. His calling includes confronting nations and kingdoms with prophetic judgment and redemptive messaging.

reevaluation.question: How does divine predestination interact with human agency in prophetic calling?
options: God completely determines prophetic selection without human consent
Prophetic calling requires mutual cooperation between divine initiative and human response
Prophetic selection is primarily based on human spiritual readiness
Divine calling transcends human understanding of personal choice
